page: Amazon Web Services x2idn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system includes 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance supports a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU score, and Passmark database operations all placing in the top 25%. The instance also demonstrates strong performance in cryptographic tasks and static web serving throughput. However, single-core CPU benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and LLM prompt processing speeds remain in the middle 50% average tier, showing a tradeoff where parallelized and database-centric workloads perform better than raw single-threaded execution.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x2idn.16xlarge offers high resource density, combining massive DDR4 memory capacity with dedicated local NVMe storage and high network throughput. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, enterprise applications, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that require substantial RAM and fast local storage access without the need for GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services x2idn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory, a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and a 50 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and cryptographic execution, placing in the top 25% of benchmarks. Conversely, single-core performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier. This resource profile is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, large databases, and multi-threaded enterprise applications requiring high memory density and fast local storage.
